target.title: fix(memory): do not filter FTS keyword search by embedding model (#48300)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #48300 — hybrid `memory_search` was dropping FTS keyword matches when chunks were indexed under a different embedding provider/model than the active one.

- Removes `AND model = ?` from lexical FTS `MATCH` and `LIKE` fallback paths in `searchKeyword` (`extensions/memory-core`).
- Vector search (`searchVector` / `searchChunksByEmbedding`) still filters by `model` — cosine similarity across mismatched embedding spaces is meaningless.
- Supersedes closed #48328, which targeted removed `src/memory/*` paths.

## Root cause

`searchKeyword` applied `providerModel` as `AND model = ?` on every FTS query. After switching embedding models (e.g. `bge-m3` → `nomic-embed-text`), exact keyword hits like `"Clyde"` in older chunks were excluded even though FTS is purely lexical.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts` (19 passed)
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Regression: `searchKeyword cross-model FTS visibility` seeds rows with different `model` values and asserts both match `"Clyde"`

## Real behavior note

Reporter also found stale in-memory index + `sync.watch: true` as a separate empty-results workaround; this PR fixes the durable SQLite FTS predicate on current `main`.


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Hybrid `memory_search` lexical lookup returns exact FTS keyword hits across embedding model changes. A chunk indexed under an older embedding model must still be returned by FTS/hybrid keyword search for `Clyde`; only vector search remains model-scoped.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch `fix/issue-48300-fts-keyword-no-model-filter` at commit `123653f9`, using the real `extensions/memory-core` SQLite schema (`ensureMemoryIndexSchema`) and real `searchKeyword` implementation from `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.ts`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seed an in-memory OpenClaw `chunks_fts` table with three rows: `old-model-hit` containing `Clyde` with `model=bge-m3`, `new-model-hit` containing `Clyde` with `model=nomic-embed-text`, and an unrelated non-matching row. Run `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs .tmp-real-proof-48300.ts` from the PR branch to execute `searchKeyword({ query: "Clyde", ftsTokenizer: "unicode61" })` through the real memory-core code.

Evidence after fix:
```text
$ node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs .tmp-real-proof-48300.ts
schema.ftsAvailable=true
query=Clyde result_count=2
old-model-hit model=bge-m3 score=0.0000 snippet=Persona notes for Clyde the assistant
new-model-hit model=nomic-embed-text score=0.0000 snippet=Persona notes for Clyde the assistant
after_fix=PASS lexical FTS returns matches across embedding model changes
```

Observed result after fix: The keyword search returned both `old-model-hit` and `new-model-hit`, proving lexical FTS no longer filters results by the stored embedding `model`. The unrelated row was not returned. This matches the intended hybrid behavior for exact keyword hits after an embedding model switch.

What was not tested: Full interactive OpenClaw gateway/UI session with a persisted user memory database; this proof covers the active memory-core SQLite schema and `searchKeyword` path that hybrid search uses before vector/text result merging.
